title: rudgea hostmanniana subsp. freemanii
intro: Rudgea hostmanniana subsp. freemanii, also known as the Brazilian Rudgea, is a plant species within the Rubiaceae family. It is native to Brazil and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Rudgea hostmanniana subsp. freemanii is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
